steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The index harness under `/app` shows old definition locations in rename, generated-module, and linked-package runs. The Rust sources live under `/app/src` and `/app/r0` through `/app/r8`; the project builds with `cargo`. Repair it so running `bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh` writes a clean `/app/output/index-report.json` from the current TypeScript files rather than a canned report. Running the script again on unchanged inputs must produce byte-identical output.

The JSON has a top-level cases array matching the supplied traces. Each case reports name, ok, observations, and stale. Each observation reports label, symbol, found, path, source, line, epoch, reused, and fresh. The symbol field names the queried identifier. Paths and sources use absolute `/app` paths. Each observation epoch is the harness epoch at query time, and epochs are nondecreasing inside each case. A found symbol points at the live definition that the case would see after its preceding edits, with line greater than zero. A missing symbol leaves path and source empty, uses line zero, and is fresh when the miss event correctly reports that the symbol is absent. Unchanged definitions are marked as reused across unrelated edits.

The audit is clean when every case is clean, stale is empty, no deleted or previous location is reported, generated entries point back to their current source, and linked package entries follow the active target.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@01f99625b95f:/app#

Analysis: We are at a root shell in /app with no prior commands run in this session. The task is to fix the Rust-based index harness so that /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh generates /app/output/index-report.json from the current TypeScript sources (not a canned/static report), and ensure determinism (second run byte-identical). Next step is to inspect repository structure, find where the canned report is coming from, understand how the matrix script invokes the harness, then run the script to reproduce the problem and iterate.
Plan: 1) Inspect /app layout and the matrix script to see how the report is produced. 2) Locate Rust entrypoints (Cargo.toml, src/main.rs) and any code that copies/embeds a precomputed index-report.json. 3) Run the matrix script to capture current bad output and identify which cases/observations are stale. 4) Search for references to index-report.json and any fixture/canned report usage. 5) Build and run harness directly if needed, then rerun matrix script to validate output is generated from current TS files and stable across runs.

python3 - <<'PY'
import json,sys
p='/app/output/index-report.json'
obj=json.load(open(p))
print('top keys:',list(obj.keys()))
print('cases:',len(obj.get('cases',[])))
for c in obj.get('cases',[])[:5]:
    print('case',c.get('name'),'ok',c.get('ok'),'stale',len(c.get('stale',[])),'obs',len(c.get('observations',[])))
PY

steps.message:
Analysis: run-matrix.sh runs `cargo run --quiet -- /app/output/index-report.json ...traces...` and it successfully produced index-report.json, but the content points at clearly old locations (e.g. /app/fixtures/alpha/ws-old/...) and marks cases as not ok with non-empty stale arrays (even containing empty strings). ripgrep is not installed, so we need to inspect Rust sources with grep/sed. Next we should inspect src/main.rs and runner/report code to see if it is loading a canned JSON or hardcoding fixture paths (ws-old, gen-v1, etc.) rather than actually indexing current TS files. Then adjust code to generate report from live filesystem state, and ensure deterministic output (stable ordering, stable epochs).
Plan: 1) Read Rust entrypoints (main.rs, lib.rs, runner.rs, report.rs, io.rs, model.rs) to locate how observations are computed and where stale gets populated. 2) Search using grep for suspicious strings like ws-old, gen-v1, pkg-a, and for reading/writing index-report.json. 3) Check Cargo.toml dependencies/features to understand intended indexing mechanism. Based on findings, remove/replace any canned report generation and implement real trace execution and indexing against the current TypeScript files under fixtures, ensuring stable ordering and no empty-string stale entries.

Analysis: The harness is not using a canned JSON directly; it reads traces, runs a single phase function (phase_a), scans TypeScript files, and builds entries. The stale paths we saw likely come from scanning the wrong directories (e.g., including ws-old) or from incorrect handling of active workspace switching / rename / generated origin mapping. io.rs shows a scan function that gathers all .ts under a directory, sorts file paths, reads `// origin:` header to map generated files back to source, and extracts exported symbols. We need the missing part of io.rs (read_trace, apply edits?) and phase_a in r0/a.rs, which likely drives the scenario and sets which directory/slot to scan. The grep didn’t find ws-old etc in code, so those are coming from the filesystem scan. Next step: inspect full io.rs (including the truncated top) and r0/a.rs to understand how it chooses directories and why old dirs are still scanned. Also check traces format to see intended events (rename, generated-module, linked-package).
Plan: 1) View full /app/src/io.rs to see trace parsing and any functions that choose scan roots. 2) Inspect /app/r0/a.rs (phase_a) to see how events are processed, how epoch is incremented, and how it decides which directories are active. 3) Inspect additional phases in r1..r8; runner currently only calls phase_a, which is suspicious—other cases likely require other phases, and using only phase_a may cause incorrect stale behavior. 4) Inspect trace files to map case names to phases. Then adjust runner to dispatch per case/trace to correct phase(s), and fix scan root selection to avoid ws-old / stale paths and follow active target for linked packages.

arguments.keystrokes: for t in /app/traces/*.trace; do echo "--- $t"; sed -n '1,200p' "$t"; done

steps.message:
Analysis: The traces explicitly reference ws-old/gen-v1/app-old etc, so those paths are not inherently wrong; what’s wrong is that observations keep pointing at the old locations after switching slots (alpha_after still points to ws-old, beta_after still points to gen-v1). That indicates the query mechanism is returning the first-seen entry for a symbol rather than the latest active root. Also pkg slot likely needs to follow link.target resolution; io.rs has resolve_target_root, but r3_d (lift_d) probably isn’t using it, causing linked-package runs to not follow the active target. Additionally, stale currently contains empty strings, likely because cast_f returns Some("") for no issue. Next: inspect r3_d, r4_e, r5_f which implement query lookup, reuse/fresh/stale logic.
Plan: 1) Inspect r3/d.rs, r4/e.rs, r5/f.rs (and any additional modules involved) to see how entries are selected on query and how staleness is detected. 2) Fix lookup to consult the active root for the slot and choose entries under that root (and for pkg, resolve symlink target via resolve_target_root). 3) Ensure epoch in observations matches state.epoch at query time (already likely). 4) Ensure stale only contains meaningful messages; avoid pushing empty string. 5) Rebuild and rerun run-matrix.sh; then rerun again and verify byte-identical output.

Analysis: Bug is in mote_e: it uses `a.entries.iter().find(|entry| entry.symbol == b)` which returns the first ever entry with that symbol, ignoring slot and active root, so after switching roots it still points to old definition. It also sets observation.epoch to entry.epoch (time indexed), but requirement says observation epoch is harness epoch at query time. cast_f uses b.label (empty) as stale note, producing empty strings. Missing observation uses Observation::missing(b,b,...) incorrectly sets label to symbol, but then caller overwrites label via struct update; fine. We need to: 1) choose correct entry based on current active roots and preferably latest epoch <= current epoch; 2) set Observation.epoch = a.epoch at query time; 3) compute fresh/reused correctly: reused should track handle of selected definition across unrelated edits; handles currently include path so when file moves it changes; ok. 4) cast_f should emit meaningful stale message (e.g., label) and never empty. We can pass label into cast_f or create stale string in phase_a with b.left. Simpler: change cast_f to accept label &str and use it for note. Also fix mote_e to look up within the active root for relevant slot(s). Since queries don’t specify slot, select entry whose path starts with any currently active root; if multiple, prefer one with greatest epoch (most recent index) and maybe shortest path? Deterministic ordering: sort candidates by (epoch desc, path asc, line asc).
Plan: Implement proper resolution in r4/e.rs and r5/f.rs:
- In mote_e: filter entries by symbol, then prefer those that are in an active slot root (entry.path starts_with(root)), and among them pick the one with max entry.epoch; if none match active roots, fall back to latest epoch overall for that symbol (still deterministic). Set observation.epoch to a.epoch (query time). Compute fresh based on whether chosen entry is under current active root for its slot. Compute reused based on last_seen for that symbol vs chosen handle.
- In cast_f: take label string separately (or use Observation.label after caller sets it). Easiest: change cast_f signature to `cast_f(a: &State, label: &str, obs: Observation, expect_found: bool)` and return stale note as that label when stale. Update phase_a accordingly.
Then run cargo fmt (if available) and rerun run-matrix.sh twice, comparing sha256 of output to ensure byte-identical.

steps.message:
Analysis: The environment lacks apply_patch and cargo-fmt, so no code changes were made; output remains unchanged (stale contains empty strings; cases still failing), though determinism is already satisfied (sha256 identical). We must edit files using available tools (e.g., heredoc with cat > file, or use python to modify). Next steps: rewrite r4/e.rs, r5/f.rs, and update r0/a.rs using cat/heredoc. Then run cargo build/run via the matrix script and confirm cases become ok with no stale entries and correct paths/epochs.
Plan: 1) Use sed to print current contents of the target Rust files and then overwrite them with corrected versions via cat heredocs. 2) Run `cargo run --quiet -- ...` via run-matrix.sh and inspect the resulting JSON summary. 3) Validate that observations after slot switches point at the new roots (ws-new, gen-v2, app-new, alias-b resolved target), that stale arrays are empty, epochs are nondecreasing and reflect query time, and that missing observations have empty path/source and line 0. 4) Rerun the script and compare sha256 to ensure byte-identical output.

for name in ['alpha','beta','gamma','zeta','delta','epsilon']:
    c=next(x for x in obj['cases'] if x['name']==name)
    print('==',name,'==')
    for o in c['observations']:
        print(o['label'],o['symbol'],o['found'],o['path'],o['source'],o['line'],o['epoch'],o['reused'],o['fresh'])
PY

New Terminal Output:

root@01f99625b95f:/app# cat > /app/r4/e.rs <<'EOF'
> use crate::model::{Entry, Observation, State};
>
> pub fn mote_e(a: &mut State, symbol: &str, expect_found: bool) -> Observation {
>     let mut candidates: Vec<&Entry> = a
>         .entries
>         .iter()
>         .filter(|entry| entry.symbol == symbol)
>         .collect();
>
>     if candidates.is_empty() {
>         return Observation::missing(symbol, symbol, a.epoch, !expect_found);
>     }
>
>     // Prefer the definition visible from currently active roots; otherwise fall back
>     // deterministically to the latest indexed definition.
>     let in_active = |e: &&Entry| {
>         a.active
>             .get(&e.slot)
>             .is_some_and(|root| e.path.starts_with(root))
>     };
>
>     fn pick_best(items: &mut Vec<&Entry>) -> &Entry {
>         items.sort_by(|l, r| {
>             // newest epoch first, then stable by path+line
>             r.epoch
>                 .cmp(&l.epoch)
>                 .then_with(|| l.path.cmp(&r.path))
>                 .then_with(|| l.line.cmp(&r.line))
>         });
>         items[0]
>     }
>
>     let mut active_candidates: Vec<&Entry> = candidates.iter().copied().filter(in_active).collect();
>     let entry = if !active_candidates.is_empty() {
>         pick_best(&mut active_candidates)
>     } else {
>         pick_best(&mut candidates)
>     };
>
>     let reused = a
>         .last_seen
>         .get(symbol)
>         .map(|old| *old == entry.handle)
>         .unwrap_or(false);
>     a.last_seen.insert(symbol.to_string(), entry.handle);
>
>     let fresh = a
>         .active
>         .get(&entry.slot)
>         .map(|root| entry.path.starts_with(root))
>         .unwrap_or(false);
>
>     Observation {
>         label: String::new(),
>         symbol: symbol.to_string(),
>         found: true,
>         path: entry.path.clone(),
>         source: entry.source.clone(),
>         line: entry.line,
>         // Observation epoch is when the query ran.
>         epoch: a.epoch,
>         reused,
>         fresh,
>     }
> }
> EOF
root@01f99625b95f:/app# cat > /app/r5/f.rs <<'EOF'
> use crate::model::{Observation, State};
>
> pub fn cast_f(
>     _a: &State,
>     label: &str,
>     obs: Observation,
>     expect_found: bool,
> ) -> (Observation, Option<String>) {
>     if expect_found {
>         if obs.found && obs.fresh {
>             (obs, None)
>         } else {
>             (obs, Some(label.to_string()))
>         }
>     } else {
>         // For misses we only record staleness when the harness incorrectly reports a symbol.
>         if obs.found {
>             (obs, Some(label.to_string()))
>         } else {
>             (obs, None)
>         }
>     }
> }
> EOF

note\) = cast_f\(a, item, false\);/let (item, note) = cast_f(a, \&b.left, item, false);/g' /app/r0/a.rs

steps.message:
Analysis: Edits were applied, but compilation fails due to pick_best returning a reference without explicit lifetime. As a result, cargo run and run-matrix.sh did not regenerate the report; the JSON remains the old output. We need to fix r4/e.rs by either adding explicit lifetimes to pick_best or avoiding returning references via helper fn (e.g., sort then use indexing in-line). After fixing, rebuild and rerun matrix; then verify all cases ok and stale empty, and output deterministic.
Plan: 1) Rewrite /app/r4/e.rs to remove the lifetime issue by not using a nested function returning &Entry; instead, sort vectors in-place and take the first element directly. 2) Re-run cargo via run-matrix.sh to regenerate index-report.json. 3) Summarize case ok/stale and check key observations for updated paths (ws-new, gen-v2, app-new, pkg alias target). 4) Re-run matrix and sha256 compare for determinism.

>         .iter()
>         .filter(|entry| entry.symbol == symbol)
>         .collect();
>
>     if candidates.is_empty() {
>         return Observation::missing(symbol, symbol, a.epoch, !expect_found);
>     }

>     let in_active = |e: &&Entry| {

steps.message:
Analysis: We fixed slot switching for app/gen: alpha, beta, delta now ok; output is deterministic. Remaining failures: gamma and zeta for pkg-linked packages have fresh=false, causing stale on Bridge queries; epsilon miss case expects Legacy to be absent after switching to gen-v2 but harness still finds Legacy in old gen-v1 (fresh=false) and marks stale. Root cause: cast_f’s miss logic flags any found as stale (good), but mote_e is choosing a non-active definition when symbol absent from active root. For epsilon, Legacy is not in gen-v2, so mote_e should return missing (fresh miss) rather than falling back to older definition. Similarly for pkg: fresh is false because State.active for slot 'pkg' stores the shown alias path, but collect_entries indexed actual resolved target path, so entry.path doesn’t start with shown root. We should store actual resolved root in active for slot pkg so fresh becomes true, and for non-pkg perhaps keep as is. Also update mote_e selection: if expect_found is false (miss), only consider active-visible candidates; if none, return missing. For expect_found=true, prefer active; if none active but exists elsewhere, that indicates stale and should return the best non-active (so stale can be reported).
Plan: 1) Update r3/d.rs so a.active for pkg stores the actual resolved root (not the alias path), ensuring fresh is computed correctly for linked package queries. 2) Update r4/e.rs: when expect_found==false, do not fall back to non-active candidates; return missing if symbol not present in active roots. When expect_found==true, keep current fallback behavior. 3) Rerun run-matrix.sh and verify all cases ok and stale empty. 4) Rerun again to confirm byte-identical output.

> use crate::io::{collect_entries, resolve_target_root};
> use crate::model::State;

> pub fn lift_d(a: &mut State, slot: &str, root: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
>     a.epoch += 1;
>
>     // For linked packages, the active root should follow the resolved target, since
>     // entries are indexed from that location.
>     let actual = resolve_target_root(root)?;
>     a.active.insert(slot.to_string(), actual.clone());
>
>     let mut found = collect_entries(a, slot, &actual, a.epoch)?;
>     a.entries.append(&mut found);
>     Ok(())
> }

steps.message:
Analysis: Compilation now fails with Rust borrow checker errors in r4/e.rs: we hold immutable borrows into a.entries (Vec<&Entry>) and then call entry_observation which mutably borrows State to update last_seen. Need to avoid holding references while mutating state. Solution: choose an entry by cloning the needed fields (path/source/line/epoch/handle/slot) into locals or a lightweight struct, then drop the candidates vector (or scope it) before mutating state. We also still need the miss behavior change and pkg active root change (r3/d.rs already updated). Next: rewrite mote_e to select a candidate as owned data (e.g., (slot, path, source, line, handle)), then update last_seen and compute fresh. Then rebuild and rerun matrix; should fix epsilon (miss) and gamma/zeta fresh for pkg due to active root now matching actual target.
Plan: Rewrite /app/r4/e.rs so it:
- First computes selected entry info without mutating State: iterate over a.entries and compute best candidate indices/handles based on active roots and expect_found.
- Store selected fields in owned Strings/usize/u64.
- Then update last_seen and build Observation.
This avoids borrowing issues. Then run run-matrix.sh and verify all cases ok with empty stale. Finally verify determinism with sha256 across two runs.

> use crate::model::{Entry, Observation, State};
>
> #[derive(Clone)]
> struct Pick {
>     slot: String,
>     path: String,
>     source: String,
>     line: usize,
>     epoch: u64,
>     handle: u64,
> }
>
> fn better(a: &Pick, b: &Pick) -> bool {
>     // Prefer newer epoch; tie-break deterministically.
>     (a.epoch, &a.path, a.line) > (b.epoch, &b.path, b.line)
> }
>
> pub fn mote_e(a: &mut State, symbol: &str, expect_found: bool) -> Observation {
>     let mut best_active: Option<Pick> = None;
>     let mut best_any: Option<Pick> = None;
>
>     for entry in &a.entries {
>         if entry.symbol != symbol {
>             continue;
>         }
>
>         let pick = Pick {
>             slot: entry.slot.clone(),
>             path: entry.path.clone(),
>             source: entry.source.clone(),
>             line: entry.line,
>             epoch: entry.epoch,
>             handle: entry.handle,
>         };
>
>         if best_any.as_ref().is_none_or(|old| better(&pick, old)) {
>             best_any = Some(pick.clone());
>         }
>
>         let is_active = a
>             .active
>             .get(&entry.slot)
>             .is_some_and(|root| entry.path.starts_with(root));
>         if is_active {
>             if best_active.as_ref().is_none_or(|old| better(&pick, old)) {
>                 best_active = Some(pick);
>             }
>         }
>     }
>
>     // For misses, only report a hit if it's visible from active roots.
>     let chosen = if expect_found {
>         best_active.or(best_any)
>     } else {
>         best_active
>     };
>
>     let Some(chosen) = chosen else {
>         return Observation::missing(symbol, symbol, a.epoch, !expect_found || true);
>     };
>
>     let reused = a
>         .last_seen
>         .get(symbol)
>         .map(|old| *old == chosen.handle)
>         .unwrap_or(false);
>     a.last_seen.insert(symbol.to_string(), chosen.handle);
>
>     let fresh = a
>         .active
>         .get(&chosen.slot)
>         .map(|root| chosen.path.starts_with(root))
>         .unwrap_or(false);
>
>     Observation {
>         label: String::new(),
>         symbol: symbol.to_string(),
>         found: true,
>         path: chosen.path,
>         source: chosen.source,
>         line: chosen.line,
>         epoch: a.epoch,
>         reused,
>         fresh,
>     }
> }
> EOF

Analysis: The harness now builds and `bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh` generates /app/output/index-report.json deterministically from the current TypeScript fixtures. All cases (alpha, beta, gamma, delta, epsilon, zeta) report ok=true with empty stale arrays. The epsilon miss is correctly represented (found=false, empty path/source, line=0, fresh=true). A previous compile warning was removed and the output hash is stable.
Plan: No further changes required; task requirements are satisfied and verified via repeated runs and sha256 stability.
